beats.activity: Wake up, shower, make espresso at home
beats.reasoning: Night owl routine: wakes later than most, needs espresso ritual to start the day. Yesterday's energy was solid but moderate, so a calm, unhurried morning fits.

beats.activity: Drive to Renton garage, SR-520 eastbound closed so taking I-90 detour
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closure 9am–noon forces early departure via I-90; adds ~15 min buffer to avoid the snarl. Leaving by 7:45 clears before peak gridlock.

beats.activity: Diagnostics and engine work at the garage in steady rain
beats.reasoning: Morning work block: grey, drizzly weather keeps foot traffic low, good for focused mechanical work. Rain doesn't affect garage interior. Yesterday's diagnostic satisfaction carries over.

beats.activity: Grab sandwich and coffee at the deli, eat in the truck
beats.reasoning: Same lunch spot as yesterday—Divya likes routine and the deli's familiar order. Rain outside means quick in-and-out; eats in truck to stay dry and unbothered.

beats.activity: Afternoon work: tire rotations, brake pads, and a clutch replacement
beats.reasoning: Steady afternoon block. Rain clears by 2pm but day stays grey and cool; perfect conditions to stay inside working, which Divya prefers over rushing home early.

beats.activity: Drive home from Renton via I-90, post-rain traffic lighter now
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure ended at noon, but Divya sticks with I-90 return—familiar route, no stress. Leaves at a normal hour; evening energy is moderate after a solid work day.

beats.activity: Change clothes, shower off grease, settle in at home
beats.reasoning: Post-work wind-down: grimy hands need attention. Divya's a night owl, so evening is just beginning. No rush to go anywhere; mood is neutral-to-content.

beats.activity: Develop film prints in home darkroom, listen to podcasts; Make dinner, scroll through photos, text with close friend about weekend plans
beats.reasoning: Divya loves film photography; rainy evening is perfect for darkroom work indoors. No trail running tonight—weather's clearing but she's not feeling the energy push like yesterday. Introspective night in.

beats.activity: Wind down, read in bed, sleep
beats.reasoning: Night owl sleeps later than most; 7.75 hours of rest spans into next morning. Ends at 06:00 as required, leaving the visual bar overnight on the map.

diary: Woke up to that grey drizzle and knew it'd be one of those days. The SR-520 closure meant I had to snake through I-90 instead—added twenty minutes to my commute, but I'd already braced for it. The garage was slick and humid all morning; I was elbow-deep in diagnostics while rain drummed on the tin roof. Grabbed a sandwich at the deli downtown around noon, ate it in the truck watching the water stream down the windshield. Afternoon flew by—tire rotations, brake work, a clutch replacement that kept my hands busy until nearly three. Traffic heading back home was actually lighter than I expected. Showered off the grease and spent the evening in my darkroom, finally developing some prints I'd been sitting on. Made pasta, caught up with Maya about weekend plans. There's something settling about a rainy day like this—productive but unhurried.
thought_process: The bridge closure threw a wrench in the morning, but I've learned to roll with Seattle traffic surprises. I wasn't stressed about it, just adjusted. Work was steady, which I appreciated—those diagnostic puzzles keep my mind sharp. I think the rain actually helped my mood, oddly enough; it gave the day a contained, focused feel. By evening, I needed the darkroom time more than usual. Something about the monotony of the afternoon work made me crave the creative side of things. Maya and I texting about plans reminded me I need to balance the grind with actual downtime. The day felt balanced, honestly.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
